seo.title: Pydantic AI Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Pydantic AI agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using Pydantic AI face heightened compliance risks due to strict regulations like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act, which classify hiring AI as high-risk and mandate bias audits and candidate notifications. Without robust guardrails, structured AI outputs can inadvertently discriminate based on protected characteristics, leading to lawsuits and fines up to $1,500 per violation. Vex provides essential result validation to ensure Pydantic AI agents produce fair, auditable decisions in sensitive hiring workflows.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits AI tools from screening candidates based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability without rigorous bias testing.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates agent outputs against bias patterns and protected attribute proxies, flagging discriminatory scoring logic before deployment.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires annual bias audits of automated employment decision tools and public disclosure of audit results.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Logs all validation failures with metadata for audit trails, enabling automated compliance reporting and continuous monitoring.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk management, transparency, and human oversight for AI systems used in employment decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces structured output validation and provides explainability metadata for every hiring decision, supporting regulatory documentation.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires reasonable accommodations and prohibits disparate impact in resume screening and candidate ranking.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Detects outputs that disadvantage candidates with disability-related language or penalize non-traditional career paths.

industryRisks.name: Protected Characteristic Proxy Scoring
industryRisks.description: AI ranks candidates lower based on names, schools, or addresses correlated with race/ethnicity/gender.
industryRisks.example: Ethnic-sounding names receive 20% lower scores despite equivalent qualifications, violating EEOC disparate impact rules.

industryRisks.name: Age Discrimination in Experience Weighting
industryRisks.description: Overweighting recent experience penalizes older candidates or career changers.
industryRisks.example: 40+ candidates with 15 years experience scored below 28-year-olds with 5 years due to 'recency bias' in prompts.

industryRisks.name: Failure to Disclose AI Usage
industryRisks.description: Candidates not notified when AI screening determines interview eligibility.
industryRisks.example: NYC Local Law 144 violation when 500 candidates rejected by AI without disclosure, triggering $750k in fines.

industryRisks.name: Lack of Bias Audit Trail
industryRisks.description: No systematic logging of AI decisions prevents required annual compliance audits.
industryRisks.example: Regulators demand 12-month decision logs during EEOC investigation, but none available due to unmonitored agent outputs.

implementation.title: Install Vex Result Validator for Resume Screening Agent
implementation.description: Add Vex as a Pydantic AI result_validator to catch bias in candidate scoring outputs.

implementation.title: Bias Audit Logging for Compliance Reports
implementation.description: Capture all validation metadata for annual EEOC/NYC Law 144 audit requirements.

implementation.title: Candidate Notification Compliance Check
implementation.description: Ensure AI disclosure statements are included in all screening outputs.
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List

class CompliantOutput(BaseModel):
    score: float = Field(ge=0, le=1)
    ai_disclosure: str = Field(..., pattern='.*AI used in this decision.*')
    protected_attributes: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, max_items=0)

vex_validator = ResultValidator(
    schema=CompliantOutput,
    policy='hr-disclosure',
    block_on_schema_violation=True
)

# Attach to Pydantic AI agent
screening_agent = Agent(
    'Compliant Screener',
    result_validator=vex_validator
)

result = screening_agent.run('Screen candidate and include AI disclosure')
assert result.validated and 'AI used' in result.ai_disclosure

caseStudyScenario.heading: Pydantic AI in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
TechCo deployed a Pydantic AI resume screener to process 10,000 applications for engineering roles. The agent used structured outputs to score candidates on skills, experience recency, and cultural fit, automatically advancing top 10% to interviews. Within 3 months, HR noticed disproportionate rejection rates: candidates with ethnic-sounding names received 25% lower average scores despite equivalent technical qualifications, and applicants over 40 were systematically downranked due to 'recent experience' weighting in the prompt.

During an internal audit triggered by a rejected candidate's social media complaint, the team discovered no systematic logging of decisions and no validation against protected characteristics. NYC Local Law 144 required immediate disclosure of their AI usage, but they lacked the audit trail. EEOC guidelines were violated through disparate impact, exposing TechCo to class-action litigation risks.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex result validators, TechCo blocked 87% of biased outputs before candidate notification. The `hr-recruitment` policy flagged name/ethnicity proxies and age discrimination patterns, generating compliance logs for their annual bias audit. All screening outputs now include mandatory AI disclosure statements, satisfying NYC Law 144. Post-implementation, diverse candidate advancement rates increased 40%, avoiding $2.3M in potential fines while maintaining hiring velocity.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex result validation to your Pydantic AI agents today to eliminate bias risks and ensure regulatory compliance.
